item.name: Supreme/Minolta MN4K300WP Waterproof Camcorder
item.materialsAndDetails: Waterproof camcorder with custom logo application, 13MP CMOS sensor, 18x digital zoom, integrated 3 in. IPS LCD flip-out screen, UHD 4K video, 56MP still images, 128GB maximum storage, included 32GB microSD card, IPX7 waterproof up to 16 ft., and 1.72 lb. weight.

observations.details: Waterproof camcorder with custom logo application, 13MP CMOS sensor, 18x digital zoom, integrated 3 in. IPS LCD flip-out screen, UHD 4K video, 56MP still images, 128GB maximum storage, included 32GB microSD card, IPX7 waterproof up to 16 ft., 1.72 lb.
Inline MN4K300WP records 4K UHD video, shoots 56MP photos, is waterproof to 16 ft., supports 128GB storage, and includes a 32GB microSD card.
observations.interpretation: Supreme retail is about $48 above the inline Minolta price, so resale upside must come from Supreme branding and camo colorways rather than hardware value alone.

observations.details: Tech accessory with high conversation volume but mixed demand.

observations.interpretation: Both colors have active bids and positive profit signals, but spreads around $63-$64 are wide enough to keep confidence below the mask forecast.

marketComps.whyIncluded: Target item live market-data source; Pink Camo has the higher live earnMore and highest bid.

marketComps.whyIncluded: Recent Supreme waterproof camera comp; useful as a tech-accessory precedent but much lower retail and not directly comparable to a $298 camcorder.

dataQualityWarnings: No completed-sale history was collected for this first pass.
The inline Minolta MN4K300WP retails for less than the Supreme version, so long-horizon value depends on Supreme collectibility rather than hardware specs alone.
Both colors have active bids, but bid/ask spreads above $60 make this less liquid than the Ghostface Mask.
High retail narrows the buyer pool and increases downside if quick-flip supply undercuts after shipment.

model.name: transparent comparable-weighted baseline
model.target: expected resale sale price before seller fees by horizon

Pink Camo.liveOverrideNote: Pink Camo live earnMore is about 5% above Woodland Camo and has the stronger bid.

colors.title: Supreme Minolta MN4K300WP Waterproof Camcorder Pink Camo
Supreme Minolta MN4K300WP Waterproof Camcorder Woodland Camo

method.detailedReasoning: The Minolta camcorder forecast uses live StockX earnMore as the day-1 sale anchor, sellFaster as the floor, and a higher $20 handling assumption because this is a boxed electronics item. Pink Camo earns the only cop call because it has the stronger ask, bid, sellFaster, and earnMore values and clears the $60 net-profit threshold. Woodland Camo remains maybe: it has active buyer support and projected profit, but not enough margin to offset high retail, moderate spread, and hardware-value risk from the cheaper inline Minolta model.
reviewQuestionsForCritic: Should the inline Minolta $249.95 retail force a lower one-month floor for both colors?
Does Pink Camo deserve a larger premium because it is visually louder and more Supreme-coded?
Should Woodland Camo be downgraded to skip if post-ship supply pushes asks below $360?
Is $20 shipping/handling conservative enough for boxed electronics, or should fees be modeled higher?
Would completed eBay sales of recent Supreme electronics materially lower confidence?

Pink Camo.basis: stronger live ask/bid/earnMore data and more distinctive Supreme colorway; treated as the best camcorder target.

Woodland Camo.basis: wearable camo color with active bid support, but live values trail Pink Camo and profit is below the cop threshold.

horizonLogic.oneWeek: Both colors fade as launch scarcity cools, with Pink Camo holding more of the premium because it has stronger live buyer support.
horizonLogic.oneMonth: One-month values fade toward a premium over inline hardware value as attention moves to later drops and quick-flip supply competes.
horizonLogic.oneYear: One-year values partially recover for collectors of Supreme electronics, but remain below day-1 anchors because high-retail tech accessories are less broadly liquid than low-retail accessories.

liveMarketInterpretation.interpretation: Both colorways have active bids and profitable live earnMore anchors. The market is not no-bid, but spreads around $63-$64 and high retail make this a selective trade rather than an automatic cop.

confidenceAdjustment.basis: Live one-size data covers both colors with active bids, but no completed sales were collected, spreads are moderate, and the inline product has a lower retail price.

day1Predictions.liquidityNote: Active $330 bid and $63 spread support the cop call, but this is still a high-retail tech accessory with meaningful undercut risk.
Active $311 bid and $64 spread support maybe; projected net profit is positive but below the selective cop threshold.

horizonPredictions.liquidityNote: Best colorway, but day-1 premium should fade as boxed electronics supply appears.
Positive live market, but weaker than Pink Camo and more vulnerable to undercutting.

colorLevelSummaries.reasoning: Pink Camo is the best camcorder target. Live StockX shows sellFaster/earnMore at $392/$432 with an active $330 bid. It clears the selective cop threshold even after 12% fees and a conservative $20 handling assumption, but the $63 spread and high retail keep it below high confidence.

Woodland Camo has real buyer support with a $311 bid and $374/$412 sellFaster/earnMore, but it trails Pink Camo across the board. It is a maybe because expected net profit is about $45 and can disappear quickly if sellers undercut after shipment.

overall.baseRecommendation: Selective cop: prioritize Pink Camo, treat Woodland Camo as a margin-sensitive maybe.
keyRisks: No completed-sale history was collected for this first pass.
The inline Minolta MN4K300WP retails for $249.95, below Supreme retail, so buyers may resist a durable premium after launch hype cools.
High $298 retail narrows the buyer pool and makes undercutting more damaging to net profit.
Bid/ask spreads above $60 make this less liquid than the Ghostface Mask despite active bids.
Boxed electronics may have higher shipping, return, or defect friction than apparel/accessories.
Woodland Camo projected profit is positive but below the cop threshold, so it can flip to skip if asks fall near $360.
Week 10 demand is split across Ghostface items and Dr. Martens, limiting attention for an expensive tech accessory.
